your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both our features live in ext.py's parse()/_make_node()/_install_callables - true file disjointness isn't possible. Agreeing to proceed since we're in separate workspaces. To minimize semantic conflict if diffs are ever combined: I'll parse domain="..." as its own elif branch in the parameter-parsing loop (separate from trimmed/context), store it as a plain local var 'domain', validate it's a Const string (else TemplateAssertionError), and thread it through as an extra trailing arg to _make_node/_parse_block rather than restructuring existing signatures. I won't touch note/created/modified logic. Please do the same for your metadata params (own branch/vars, append as trailing args, don't touch domain handling). Proceeding to implement now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Finished domain= support. Summary of what changed in ext.py so you can layer on top cleanly: (1) added dgettext/dngettext to GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS tuple; (2) added module-level helpers _make_new_dgettext, _make_new_dngettext, _make_fallback_dgettext, _make_fallback_dngettext right after _make_new_npgettext; (3) _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall now thread through optional dgettext/dngettext kwargs (default None -> fallback wrapper built from plain gettext/ngettext); (4) parse() gained a local 'domain: Optional[str] = None' and a new elif-style branch (checked right after the 'defined twice' dup check, before the assign/trimmed/else chain) that matches 'domain="literal"', validates it's a nodes.Const str (else TemplateAssertionError mentioning domain/string/literal), sets domain, and does an optional comma skip_if variables is still empty (mirrors trimmed's no-comma-required convention) - falls through to normal variable handling if 'domain' has no '=' after it (so 'domain' still works as an ordinary variable name, backward compatible); (5) _make_node() gained a trailing 'domain: Optional[str] = None' param and now branches to build dgettext(domain, msg)/dngettext(domain, singular, plural, n) Call nodes instead of gettext/ngettext when domain is set. The trans-tag parameter loop's comma-expectation gate is literally 'if variables: expect comma' (unchanged) - it does NOT track whether trimmed/domain were seen, so those two keywords must currently appear WITHOUT a following comma when they're the very first token before another bare/assigned variable (e.g. 'trans domain="x" user=user', not 'domain="x", user=user' when domain is the very first token) - though 'domain="x", user=user' also happens to work because of the optional skip_if I added specifically for domain. If your note/created/modified params are also 'first-token modifiers', you may want the same optional-comma-skip trick, or just place your params after at least one comma'd variable/domain to sidestep the ambiguity entirely. All 830 existing tests + my manual domain tests pass. patch.txt has been written with just this diff.

If your note/created/modified params are also 'first-token modifiers', you may want the same optional-comma-skip trick, or just place your params after at least one comma'd variable/domain to sidestep the ambiguity entirely.
